← All worlds/World 07
World 07 · 1 state

Existential Confusion

Films for the moment the floor of certainty disappears — and the strange, vertigo-filled task of building a meaning that still holds you.

Choose an emotion
Meaning of Life
On Meaning of Life

The question arrived sometime after you were old enough to understand it and before you were ready to live with it. Why any of this? What makes a life matter? What do you owe the time you have been given? Philosophy courses give you frameworks. These films give you something different — they give you the question, held in a human life, breathed through an hour of real consequences. The best of them do not answer. They sit beside you in the not-knowing and demonstrate, by their very existence, that the asking is worthwhile.

Meaning of Life

Films for the long stare at the size of the universe.

23Curated films

23 films — quick browse

CS8.9

The Tree of Life

Terrence Malick · 2011

🏆 Palme d'Or Cannes

Synecdoche, New York

Charlie Kaufman · 2008

🏆 National Society of Film Critics Best Film

Everything Everywhere All at Once

Daniels · 2022

🏆 Won 7 Academy Awards incl. Best Picture

Soul

🏆 Won 2 Academy Awards

Stalker

🏆 FIPRESCI Prize Cannes 1980

The Seventh Seal

The Turin Horse

🏆 Colombia's first Academy Award nomination

CS8.9
2011

The Tree of Life

Terrence Malick

View Film →

A Texas family in the 1950s and their oldest son’s adult life, intercut with the formation of the universe itself. Malick at his most ambitious.

CS8.7
2008

Synecdoche, New York

Charlie Kaufman

View Film →

A theatre director awarded a vast grant tries to build a life-size replica of his life inside a New York warehouse. The project, like its maker, never finishes.

CS9.1
2022

Everything Everywhere All at Once

Daniels

View Film →

A laundromat owner in the middle of a tax audit discovers she’s the only person who can save the multiverse — by accessing every life she didn’t live.

CS9.0
2020

Soul

Pete Docter

View Film →

A middle-school music teacher who finally lands the gig of his dreams dies on the same day — and finds himself in a celestial waystation, trying to get back.

CS9.5
1979

Stalker

Andrei Tarkovsky

View Film →

In an unnamed country, a guide leads two clients into the forbidden Zone toward a room said to grant the visitor’s deepest wish.

CS9.4
1957

The Seventh Seal

Ingmar Bergman

View Film →

A knight returning from the Crusades plays chess with Death across a plague-stricken Sweden, trying to buy time to do one meaningful thing.

CS8.3
2001

Waking Life

Richard Linklater

View Film →

A young man drifts through a series of conversations — with philosophers, dreamers, strangers — unsure whether he’s awake or trapped in a long dream.

CS8.0
2009

Enter the Void

Gaspar Noé

View Film →

A small-time drug dealer in Tokyo is shot in a club bathroom — and his consciousness floats above the city, observing his sister, his past, his death.

CS8.4
2009

Mr. Nobody

Jaco Van Dormael

View Film →

The last mortal man on Earth, aged 118, recounts every possible life he could have lived from the moment his parents separated when he was nine.

CS8.6
2017

A Ghost Story

David Lowery

View Film →

After a young musician dies, his ghost — a figure under a sheet — lingers in the house he shared with his wife, watching time pass without him.

CS9.7
1968

2001: A Space Odyssey

Stanley Kubrick

View Film →

A prehistoric ape tribe. A voyage to Jupiter. A malevolent computer. A starchild. Kubrick’s film about the meaning of intelligence and the size of the universe.

CS9.0
1982

Blade Runner

Ridley Scott

View Film →

A detective in 2019 Los Angeles hunts down rogue replicants. The film asks what it means to be real, and what memory means to identity.

CS8.5
1997

Contact

Robert Zemeckis

View Film →

A SETI scientist receives a signal from deep space. What follows is not about the aliens but about the meaning of belief.

CS7.9
2012

Cloud Atlas

Wachowskis & Tom Tykwer

View Film →

Six stories across six centuries, intercut and interconnected. About the same soul in different bodies making the same choices.

CS8.1
2013

Coherence

James Ward Byrkit

View Film →

During a comet passing, a dinner party discovers there may be other versions of themselves in parallel houses on the same street.

CS7.8
2011

Another Earth

Mike Cahill

View Film →

A second Earth appears in the sky. A young woman who caused a car accident tries to make amends with the man whose family she killed.

CS8.5
2013

Upstream Color

Shane Carruth

View Film →

A man and a woman are connected by a lifecycle that neither of them fully understands. The film is the attempt to make sense of it.

CS8.9
2011

Take Shelter

Jeff Nichols

View Film →

A man in Ohio begins having apocalyptic visions and obsessively builds a storm shelter. His wife tries to understand whether he is prophetic or ill.

CS8.8
2012

The Master

Paul Thomas Anderson

View Film →

A rootless drifter after WWII becomes attached to a charismatic leader of a philosophical movement. The film is about the space between them.

CS8.0
2013

Gravity

Alfonso Cuarón

View Film →

Two astronauts survive a debris strike that destroys their shuttle. The film is the attempt to return to Earth from deep space alone.

CS8.0
2014

I Origins

Mike Cahill

View Film →

A molecular biologist studying eyes finds scientific evidence that may challenge materialist assumptions about consciousness and reincarnation.

CS8.8
2011

The Turin Horse

Béla Tarr

View Film →

Based on what Nietzsche may have seen when he collapsed weeping on a Turin street, a farmer and his daughter live six days of slow, grinding existence.

CS8.7
2015

Embrace of the Serpent

Ciro Guerra

View Film →

Two expeditions into the Colombian Amazon decades apart, both seeking a sacred healing plant. Told in black-and-white.

Grief →

For the love that had to end.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.

18 emotional states·277 curated films·Growing weekly
